context.question: Will Javokhir Sindarov win the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament?
context.market_title: Will Javokhir Sindarov win the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament?

decision.primary_crux: This remains a bounded price refresh rather than a factual regime change: Sindarov still appears overwhelmingly likely to win, but without independently verified official clinch or winner confirmation the market's 99.05% price is above what the evidence supports.
secondary_cruxes: The compact refresh brief indicates a material price move, not new official evidence, so the prior factual crux is largely unchanged.

The strongest bounded evidence still points to Sindarov leading by two points after round 12 with two rounds left and being guaranteed at least a playoff, which supports a very high probability but not near-certainty.
Because a clean official FIDE standings/clinch page remains missing, residual competitive, playoff, and procedural tails remain economically meaningful at a 99% quote.

valuation.compression_reason: No new primary-source confirmation was added in this bounded refresh, so the proper update is to keep the prior high-Yes estimate broadly intact and mark the market as having repriced above it.

execution_semantics.time_horizon: Round 13 onward through official FIDE result declaration

portfolio_constraints: Do not initiate fresh exposure from this packet because the market has repriced above independently justified fair value.
Existing aligned exposure may be held, but do not add unless refreshed official evidence shows an actual clinch or the market cheapens materially.
Refresh on any official standings/result update before taking action because freshness remains decision-relevant.

bands.name: max_enter
bands.notes: No autonomous entry from this packet; a much cheaper market would require a fresh official-state review.

bands.name: scaled_enter
bands.notes: No autonomous entry authorized because the current bounded evidence is not stronger than the market move.

bands.name: hold
bands.notes: Hold-only zone consistent with a very likely Sindarov win but insufficient confidence for fresh adds.

bands.name: trim
bands.notes: Trim/avoid increasing as price moves above the upper end of justified fair value.

bands.name: exit
bands.notes: Exit/avoid at extreme prices because residual two-round, playoff, and official-declaration tails still remain.

thesis_breakers: Official FIDE standings/results show Sindarov's lead is smaller than reported or he fails to convert a key round.
A rival closes the gap materially or forces a more complex playoff path than currently assumed.
Official reporting contradicts the secondary reporting that he is guaranteed at least a playoff.
market_structure_breakers: A clean official FIDE standings/clinch page materially changes the live state relative to the current bounded interpretation.
The market reprices sharply again on actual round results or official declaration, making this refresh packet stale immediately.
A rules or tiebreak clarification changes the conversion odds from the current late-stage lead.
time_breakers: This packet expires at valid_until unless refreshed with later round results or official FIDE standings.
Once an official winner is declared, this in-progress packet should be replaced immediately.
reversal_conditions: Reverse only on a new packet after refreshed official results show a materially worse position for Sindarov.
Do not auto-reverse from this packet.
Suspend this packet if official and secondary reporting diverge materially.

key_uncertainties: A clean official FIDE live standings/clinch page still was not independently captured.
Two rounds and possible playoff/tiebreak mechanics still leave a nonzero failure path.
The market may be incorporating stronger live-state information than was recoverable in this bounded refresh.
reasons_to_pass: The market move itself does not invalidate the prior thesis; it mainly removes the prior valuation cushion.
At 99.05%, even small residual tails matter economically.
The bounded refresh still did not produce enough new primary-source evidence to justify following the market to near-certainty.
what_would_change_my_mind: A clean official FIDE confirmation that Sindarov has clinched would move fair value materially closer to the market price.
A round result or official standings update showing the race reopened would push fair value materially lower.
A materially cheaper market price without loss of the current late-stage lead would restore a potential value case.

audit.one_sentence_rationale: Sindarov remains very likely to win, but because the move to 99.05% is not matched by independently verified official-state improvement, the prior edge has inverted and the disciplined stance is watch-only rather than chasing near-certainty.
audit.notes: This refresh is still bounded and lightweight; no new independently verified official evidence was added, so the update is about market repricing rather than a changed underlying thesis.
